qa_pairs.question: How can users programmatically obtain a list of GPU model specifiers, including Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) specifiers, available on a specific cluster within Slurm?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can obtain GPU model specifiers by running the Slurm command: `sinfo -o "%G"|grep gpu|sed 's/gpu://g'|sed 's/),/\n/g'|cut -d: -f1|sort|uniq`. However, this command will not provide short synonyms available at some sites, and the presence of a GPU model does not guarantee its availability for all jobs, as there may be further restrictions based on factors like research group.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it strongly recommended to always provide a specific GPU model specifier in Slurm job scripts?
qa_pairs.answer: It is strongly recommended to always provide a specific GPU model specifier because if one is not supplied, the job may either be rejected or sent to an arbitrary GPU instance. Since very few programs can efficiently use an arbitrary GPU, this ensures more efficient and successful job execution.

qa_pairs.question: What are the recommended maximum CPU core allocations for each full GPU requested on the Fir, Narval, Nibi, and Rorqual clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: For each full GPU requested, it is recommended to allocate no more than 12 CPU cores on Fir, no more than 12 CPU cores on Narval, no more than 14 CPU cores on Nibi, and no more than 16 CPU cores on Rorqual.
